winner: I was building a bookshelf with my laptop and hammer when suddenly it started singing opera—turns out, I'd accidentally designed a musical instrument out of plywood and RAM.

candidates.text: Why did I bring a hammer to my laptop repair? Because the technician said it was a nail-biter, but then he started debugging and I realized it was actually a *screwdriver* moment!

candidates.text: I tried to use my laptop as a hammer, but it refused to cooperate—apparently, it's not in the mood for any kind of work.

candidates.text: My IT guy told me that if my laptop is acting up, I should try to give it a little tap, so I grabbed a hammer and tried to improve its performance by hitting it until it started making a lot more noise.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses both required words (hammer, laptop), and delivers a surprising twist with the literal interpretation of 'tap' as hitting with a hammer. It's specific and funny, though slightly predictable in its setup."}
